title: lichen_lora_l2_tx takes non-const data pointer
description:
lichen_lora_l2_tx() takes uint8_t *data instead of const uint8_t *data, even though the function does not modify the buffer.

Location:
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.h:114
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:313

Signature: int lichen_lora_l2_tx(uint8_t *data, size_t len);

The doc comment says 'may be mutated by driver' which explains the non-const, but this is propagating Zephyr's lora_send() signature which also takes non-const. The LICHEN code itself does not mutate the buffer.

What goes wrong: Callers with const data must cast away const to call this function, which is a code smell. The lichen_l2.c caller (line 203, 206) uses non-const tx_frame_buf and tx_ipv6_buf so this works, but it's an unnecessary constraint.

If the Zephyr lora_send() API changes to const in the future, this should be updated. For now, the doc comment is accurate - the driver may mutate. Consider tracking this as tech debt.
close_reason: Duplicate of project-LICHEN-rwio.22. Both issues concern the same non-const data pointer in lichen_lora_l2_tx(). Fixed in .22.
